title: The Full Sea Rolls And Thunders
content:
The full sea rolls and thunders
    In glory and in glee.
    O, bury me not in the senseless earth
    But in the living sea!

    Ay, bury me where it surges
    A thousand miles from shore,
    And in its brotherly unrest
    I'll range for evermore.

    1876
meaning:
William Ernest Henley's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Full Sea Rolls And Thunders"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
